proposed_hypotheses.text: For each 1-unit increase in `feature_041`, mean `pfs_months` is higher in this cohort.

analyses.code: Univariate OLS: pfs_months ~ feature_041
analyses.result_summary: `feature_041` was strongly positively associated with `pfs_months`: slope +0.176502 months per 1-unit increase (z=266.53).

iterations.update: The largest signal was continuous and positive, so subsequent iterations screened other features for independent adverse or favorable main effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For each 1-level increase in `feature_025` from 0 to 2, mean `pfs_months` is lower.

analyses.code: Univariate OLS: pfs_months ~ feature_025; level means by feature_025
analyses.result_summary: `feature_025` had a monotone adverse association: level means were 5.271 months at 0, 4.054 at 1, and 2.908 at 2; linear slope -1.189668 months per level (z=-83.99).

iterations.update: The ordered pattern suggested `feature_025` should be retained as a main-effect covariate in later adjusted models.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_036 = 1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_036 = 0`.

analyses.code: Welch mean comparison: pfs_months by feature_036
analyses.result_summary: Mean `pfs_months` was 3.703 for `feature_036 = 1` versus 5.055 for `feature_036 = 0`; difference -1.352001 months (z=-68.49).

iterations.update: Because this binary adverse effect was large, later iterations tested whether it interacted with `feature_041`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_043 = 1` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_043 = 0`.

analyses.code: Welch mean comparison: pfs_months by feature_043
analyses.result_summary: Mean `pfs_months` was 5.089 for `feature_043 = 1` versus 4.117 for `feature_043 = 0`; difference +0.972162 months (z=32.02).

iterations.update: The favorable `feature_043` signal became the focus of subgroup and interaction testing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For each 1-unit increase in `feature_056`, mean `pfs_months` is lower.

analyses.code: Univariate OLS: pfs_months ~ feature_056
analyses.result_summary: `feature_056` was inversely associated with `pfs_months`: slope -0.073015 months per 1-unit increase (z=-27.68).

iterations.update: This continuous adverse predictor was included in the multivariable main-effect model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For each 1-unit increase in `feature_050`, mean `pfs_months` is higher.

analyses.code: Univariate OLS: pfs_months ~ feature_050
analyses.result_summary: `feature_050` was positively associated with `pfs_months`: slope +0.465363 months per 1-unit increase (z=22.87).

iterations.update: The positive direction was carried forward into the adjusted model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_001 = 1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_001 = 0`.

analyses.code: Welch mean comparison: pfs_months by feature_001
analyses.result_summary: Mean `pfs_months` was 4.121 for `feature_001 = 1` versus 4.448 for `feature_001 = 0`; difference -0.326773 months (z=-16.14).

iterations.update: Because `feature_001` was adverse and common, it was tested as a modifier of the favorable `feature_043` association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_015 = 1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_015 = 0`.

analyses.code: Welch mean comparison: pfs_months by feature_015
analyses.result_summary: Mean `pfs_months` was 4.114 for `feature_015 = 1` versus 4.419 for `feature_015 = 0`; difference -0.305207 months (z=-14.84).

iterations.update: `feature_015` was another adverse common subgroup marker and was tested against `feature_043` in interaction analyses.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For each 1-unit increase in `feature_029`, mean `pfs_months` is lower.

analyses.code: Univariate OLS: pfs_months ~ feature_029
analyses.result_summary: `feature_029` had a smaller but significant inverse association with `pfs_months`: slope -0.005346 months per 1-unit increase (z=-8.44).

iterations.update: This weaker continuous adverse feature was retained in adjusted models to reduce confounding of stronger effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_066 = 1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_066 = 0`.

analyses.code: Welch mean comparison: pfs_months by feature_066
analyses.result_summary: Mean `pfs_months` was 4.094 for `feature_066 = 1` versus 4.322 for `feature_066 = 0`; difference -0.227870 months (z=-5.11).

iterations.update: Although less prevalent, `feature_066` was adverse and was later tested as a modifier of `feature_043`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for `feature_025`, `feature_036`, `feature_043`, `feature_056`, `feature_050`, `feature_001`, `feature_015`, `feature_029`, `feature_066`, `feature_042`, `feature_039`, `feature_028`, and `feature_012`, each 1-unit increase in `feature_041` remains associated with higher mean `pfs_months`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS main-effects model with centered covariates
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `feature_041` coefficient was +0.175842 months per unit (z=453.70), essentially unchanged from the univariate estimate.

iterations.update: The `feature_041` association was not explained by the other screened predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the screened main-effect covariates, each 1-level increase in `feature_025` remains associated with lower mean `pfs_months`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS main-effects model with centered covariates
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `feature_025` coefficient was -1.171314 months per level (z=-205.79), confirming a strong independent adverse association.

iterations.update: The ordered adverse pattern remained robust after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the screened main-effect covariates, `feature_036 = 1` remains associated with lower mean `pfs_months` than `feature_036 = 0`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS main-effects model with centered covariates
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted coefficient for `feature_036` was -1.361490 months (z=-175.61), confirming an independent adverse effect.

iterations.update: This stable main effect supported the later age-by-`feature_036` interaction test.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the screened main-effect covariates, `feature_043 = 1` remains associated with higher mean `pfs_months` than `feature_043 = 0`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS main-effects model with centered covariates
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted coefficient for `feature_043` was +0.942927 months (z=97.86), confirming a strong independent favorable association.

iterations.update: With the main effect confirmed, the next iterations focused on which subgroups retained or lost this benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the screened main-effect covariates, each 1-unit increase in `feature_056` remains associated with lower mean `pfs_months`.
After adjustment for the screened main-effect covariates, each 1-unit increase in `feature_050` remains associated with higher mean `pfs_months`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS main-effects model with centered covariates
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `feature_056` coefficient was -0.076078 months per unit (z=-76.10), supporting the adverse direction.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS main-effects model with centered covariates
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `feature_050` coefficient was +0.469208 months per unit (z=61.00), supporting the favorable direction.

iterations.update: Both continuous main effects persisted after adjustment and helped define the baseline multivariable structure.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the screened main-effect covariates, `feature_042 = 1` remains associated with higher mean `pfs_months` than `feature_042 = 0`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS main-effects model with centered covariates
analyses.result_summary: The univariate `feature_042` difference was +0.221 months, but after adjustment the coefficient was only +0.020845 months (z=0.91).

iterations.update: The adjusted result refuted an independent `feature_042` main effect, so later tests treated it as a possible modifier rather than a main driver.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable association of `feature_043 = 1` with `pfs_months` is smaller among patients with `feature_001 = 1` than among patients with `feature_001 = 0`.

analyses.code: Subgroup mean differences and adjusted OLS interaction: feature_001 * feature_043
analyses.result_summary: `feature_043` was associated with +1.656 months when `feature_001 = 0` but only +0.027 months when `feature_001 = 1`; the adjusted interaction coefficient was -1.776230 months (z=-105.03).

iterations.update: `feature_001` nearly eliminated the apparent `feature_043` benefit, indicating strong subgroup heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable association of `feature_043 = 1` with `pfs_months` is smaller among patients with `feature_015 = 1` than among patients with `feature_015 = 0`.

analyses.code: Subgroup mean differences and adjusted OLS interaction: feature_015 * feature_043
analyses.result_summary: `feature_043` was associated with +1.480 months when `feature_015 = 0` but only +0.026 months when `feature_015 = 1`; the adjusted interaction coefficient was -1.472558 months (z=-86.83).

iterations.update: A second common adverse subgroup marker also removed most of the `feature_043` benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable association of `feature_043 = 1` with `pfs_months` is smaller among patients with `feature_066 = 1` than among patients with `feature_066 = 0`.

analyses.code: Subgroup mean differences and adjusted OLS interaction: feature_066 * feature_043
analyses.result_summary: `feature_043` was associated with +1.027 months when `feature_066 = 0` but -0.156 months when `feature_066 = 1`; the adjusted interaction coefficient was -1.765140 months (z=-44.97).

iterations.update: `feature_066` marked another subgroup where the otherwise favorable `feature_043` pattern did not hold.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for screened main effects and stronger `feature_043` modifiers, `feature_042 = 1` further increases the favorable association of `feature_043 = 1` with `pfs_months`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS interaction model including feature_042 * feature_043
analyses.result_summary: The unadjusted difference-in-differences for `feature_042 * feature_043` was +0.988 months, but after adjustment the interaction coefficient was +0.067116 months (z=1.42).

iterations.update: The apparent `feature_042` amplification was not robust once stronger main effects and modifiers were included.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The joint adverse effect of `feature_001 = 1` and `feature_015 = 1` on `pfs_months` is less negative than the sum of their separate adverse effects.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS interaction model including feature_001 * feature_015
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `feature_015 * feature_001` interaction coefficient was +0.500501 months (z=36.45), indicating less-than-additive adversity for the co-occurring features.

iterations.update: The adverse binary markers interacted with each other as well as with `feature_043`, so multivariable interaction structure mattered.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_041` and `pfs_months` is weaker among patients with `feature_036 = 1` than among patients with `feature_036 = 0`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS interaction model including feature_041 * feature_036; subgroup slopes by feature_036
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_041` slope was +0.180379 months/unit when `feature_036 = 0` and +0.173255 when `feature_036 = 1`; the adjusted interaction coefficient was -0.006977 months per unit (z=-10.66).

iterations.update: `feature_036` had both a large adverse main effect and a small but significant attenuation of the `feature_041` slope.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the screened main-effect covariates, each 1-unit increase in `feature_028` remains associated with lower mean `pfs_months`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS main-effects model with centered covariates
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `feature_028` coefficient was -0.000425 months per unit (z=-8.91), supporting a small independent adverse association.

iterations.update: A small independent continuous adverse effect remained after accounting for the dominant predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the screened main-effect covariates, each 1-unit increase in `feature_039` remains associated with lower mean `pfs_months`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS main-effects model with centered covariates
analyses.result_summary: `feature_039` had a nominal univariate slope of -0.000595 months/unit, but after adjustment its coefficient was +0.000023 months/unit (z=0.29).

iterations.update: This refuted `feature_039` as an independent predictor and suggested its univariate signal was confounded.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the screened main-effect covariates, each 1-unit increase in `feature_012` remains associated with lower mean `pfs_months`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS main-effects model with centered covariates
analyses.result_summary: `feature_012` had a nominal univariate slope of -0.065312 months/unit, but after adjustment its coefficient was -0.005166 months/unit (z=-0.42).

iterations.update: This second refuted nominal signal reinforced that adjusted modeling was necessary before interpreting smaller univariate associations.
